about.heavy.band: Rural Roads is a Heavy Metal/Hard Rock band from Porto Alegre, Brazil.
about.formiga: Guilherme "Formiga" - guitar and backing vocals (founding member)
about.will: Will Guimarães - guitar and backing vocals (since 2013)
about.founded.by.formiga.and.papao: Rural Roads was founded by Formiga and Papão on October 4, 2009, in the southern outskirts of Porto Alegre, Brazil — a rural area that ultimately inspired the band’s name. The project initially aimed to perform at a local event, the Festival de Bandas Belendrinas (FBB). After about a month searching for members, the first line-up was completed:
about.debug.fbb: Just one week after forming — and with only a single full rehearsal — the band made its debut at the FBB. After the festival, the members decided to continue, and Gabryel Corrêa joined as a second guitarist. From that point on, the band began performing at festivals across Porto Alegre and its metropolitan area.
about.after.six.months: Six months later, Jet left the band and was replaced by Bu. After two years, Irwin departed due to creative differences. This led to a transitional period, during which several vocalists auditioned but did not remain in the band. During this time, guitarist Gabryel also chose to leave.
about.hiatus.period: The band entered a brief hiatus while continuing auditions, eventually finding Giancarlo, who joined as lead vocalist in early 2013. At this stage, the focus shifted toward original compositions, blending classic and modern elements of rock and metal.
about.will.guitar: Will joined later that same year, completing the guitar lineup and strengthening the songwriting process.
about.between.2013.2015: Between 2013 and 2015, the band performed numerous shows and festivals, and recorded a demo in 2014, produced independently by its members.
about.papao.exit: Papão left the band in 2015, and Mauricio Tessmer took over on drums. With this lineup, the band recorded its first full-length album, produced by Sebastian Carsin at Hurricane Studio (Porto Alegre), featuring original songs as well as reworked versions of tracks from the 2014 demo.
about.single.ride.album: On October 31, 2018, Rural Roads released its first single, “Ride”, along with a lyric video, as a preview of the upcoming album. The full album was released digitally on November 29, 2018 (with a physical edition following in 2019), featuring “Ride” and 12 additional original tracks.
about.covid: During the pandemic, the band remained active by producing “quarantine-style” videos, with each member recording their parts individually.
about.farm: In 2021, the band recorded a live-in-studio EP/video featuring five songs from their debut album at AudioFarm Studio, with Cesar Caramês on bass and Ricardo Lannes as a guest drummer. The release, titled “Farm Sessions”, came out digitally in October 2022.
about.2025: After further lineup changes, founding drummer Papão returned in 2023. The band then shifted its focus to new material. A new album is currently in the final stages of production at Hurricane Studio, once again produced by Sebastian Carsin, and now featuring Cesar Caramês as a full-time member on bass.
about.actual: With a solidified lineup, Rural Roads is back on the live circuit, performing at shows and festivals while continuing to blend classic and modern influences in its sound.
